input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A woman wearing a blue cardigan and work gloves stands beside a white washing machine with silver controls. Her mouth shape changes slightly, while her hands stay clasped together near the lid. A black toolbox sits on a surface to the right, and a repair logo appears in the top left corner against the white wall background.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual elements presented in the video introduction.

answers.text: The video begins with a woman in a blue cardigan and gloves standing beside a white washing machine, with a toolbox nearby. At 14 seconds, a title card appears reading "Replacing a Fabric Softener Dispenser." Shortly after, at 18 seconds, the scene shifts to display an image of a translucent white plastic dispenser accompanied by phonetic text below it, defining the part's function during the spin cycle before returning to the presenter.

From 26 to 28 seconds, a static white screen defines the softener dispenser with blue explanatory text and a fixed image of the translucent container. Following this, the video returns to the woman beside the washing machine from 28 to 32 seconds. She rests her gloved hands on the appliance lid while speaking, with the repair logo visible in the top left and the black toolbox stationary to her right, ending with a smile.

From 32 to 34 seconds, the woman shifts her gaze downward toward the washing machine. She firmly grips the lid with her gloved hands and lifts it open. This action reveals the interior drum and exposes a large instruction sticker adhered to the underside of the raised lid, marking a transition from the static presentation to physical interaction with the appliance.

From 34 to 36 seconds, the woman fully opens the lid, supporting it with her left hand to reveal an instructional sticker with purple and orange text. Between 36 and 38 seconds, she reaches her right hand into the drum to touch the central agitator cap while looking inside. Finally, at 38 seconds, the camera cuts to a close-up interior view showing the white agitator with a blue center as her gloved hand approaches it against the perforated metal wall.

From 42 to 44 seconds, the camera cuts from a close-up back to a medium shot of the woman. She leans forward over the open washing machine, using both gloved hands to grip the white central agitator inside the drum. She appears to twist or remove the component while looking down into the appliance, marking the start of the physical removal process.

samples.output: <think>The woman in the blue cardigan finishes pressing the white dispenser cap inside the drum. She stands upright and places her gloved right hand on the edge of the open washing machine lid, looking down at the appliance to inspect her work.</think><answer></answer>

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual elements presented in the video introduction.

answers.text: The video begins with a woman in a blue cardigan and gloves standing beside a white washing machine, with a toolbox nearby. At 14 seconds, a title card appears reading "Replacing a Fabric Softener Dispenser." Shortly after, at 18 seconds, the scene shifts to display an image of a translucent white plastic dispenser accompanied by phonetic text below it, defining the part's function during the spin cycle before returning to the presenter.

From 26 to 28 seconds, a static white screen defines the softener dispenser with blue explanatory text and a fixed image of the translucent container. Following this, the video returns to the woman beside the washing machine from 28 to 32 seconds. She rests her gloved hands on the appliance lid while speaking, with the repair logo visible in the top left and the black toolbox stationary to her right, ending with a smile.

From 32 to 34 seconds, the woman shifts her gaze downward toward the washing machine. She firmly grips the lid with her gloved hands and lifts it open. This action reveals the interior drum and exposes a large instruction sticker adhered to the underside of the raised lid, marking a transition from the static presentation to physical interaction with the appliance.

From 34 to 36 seconds, the woman fully opens the lid, supporting it with her left hand to reveal an instructional sticker with purple and orange text. Between 36 and 38 seconds, she reaches her right hand into the drum to touch the central agitator cap while looking inside. Finally, at 38 seconds, the camera cuts to a close-up interior view showing the white agitator with a blue center as her gloved hand approaches it against the perforated metal wall.

From 42 to 44 seconds, the camera cuts from a close-up back to a medium shot of the woman. She leans forward over the open washing machine, using both gloved hands to grip the white central agitator inside the drum. She appears to twist or remove the component while looking down into the appliance, marking the start of the physical removal process.

From 26 to 28 seconds, a static white screen defines the softener dispenser with blue explanatory text and a fixed image of the translucent container. Following this, the video returns to the woman beside the washing machine from 28 to 32 seconds. She rests her gloved hands on the appliance lid while speaking, with the repair logo visible in the top left and the black toolbox stationary to her right, ending with a smile.

From 32 to 34 seconds, the woman shifts her gaze downward toward the washing machine. She firmly grips the lid with her gloved hands and lifts it open. This action reveals the interior drum and exposes a large instruction sticker adhered to the underside of the raised lid, marking a transition from the static presentation to physical interaction with the appliance.

From 34 to 36 seconds, the woman fully opens the lid, supporting it with her left hand to reveal an instructional sticker with purple and orange text. Between 36 and 38 seconds, she reaches her right hand into the drum to touch the central agitator cap while looking inside. Finally, at 38 seconds, the camera cuts to a close-up interior view showing the white agitator with a blue center as her gloved hand approaches it against the perforated metal wall.

From 42 to 44 seconds, the camera cuts from a close-up back to a medium shot of the woman. She leans forward over the open washing machine, using both gloved hands to grip the white central agitator inside the drum. She appears to twist or remove the component while looking down into the appliance, marking the start of the physical removal process.

From 32 to 34 seconds, the woman shifts her gaze downward toward the washing machine. She firmly grips the lid with her gloved hands and lifts it open. This action reveals the interior drum and exposes a large instruction sticker adhered to the underside of the raised lid, marking a transition from the static presentation to physical interaction with the appliance.

From 34 to 36 seconds, the woman fully opens the lid, supporting it with her left hand to reveal an instructional sticker with purple and orange text. Between 36 and 38 seconds, she reaches her right hand into the drum to touch the central agitator cap while looking inside. Finally, at 38 seconds, the camera cuts to a close-up interior view showing the white agitator with a blue center as her gloved hand approaches it against the perforated metal wall.

From 42 to 44 seconds, the camera cuts from a close-up back to a medium shot of the woman. She leans forward over the open washing machine, using both gloved hands to grip the white central agitator inside the drum. She appears to twist or remove the component while looking down into the appliance, marking the start of the physical removal process.

From 44 to 46 seconds, the camera zooms in as gloved hands twist the top ring of the agitator. Between 46 and 48 seconds, the white ring cap is lifted off, revealing the hollow interior of the central dispenser. At 48 seconds, the woman holds the removed cap while speaking. Finally, from 50 to 52 seconds, she leans over the machine and presses the white plastic ring cap back onto the central dispenser column inside the drum.

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual elements presented in the video introduction.

answers.text: The video begins with a woman in a blue cardigan and gloves standing beside a white washing machine, with a toolbox nearby. At 14 seconds, a title card appears reading "Replacing a Fabric Softener Dispenser." Shortly after, at 18 seconds, the scene shifts to display an image of a translucent white plastic dispenser accompanied by phonetic text below it, defining the part's function during the spin cycle before returning to the presenter.

From 26 to 28 seconds, a static white screen defines the softener dispenser with blue explanatory text and a fixed image of the translucent container. Following this, the video returns to the woman beside the washing machine from 28 to 32 seconds. She rests her gloved hands on the appliance lid while speaking, with the repair logo visible in the top left and the black toolbox stationary to her right, ending with a smile.

From 32 to 34 seconds, the woman shifts her gaze downward toward the washing machine. She firmly grips the lid with her gloved hands and lifts it open. This action reveals the interior drum and exposes a large instruction sticker adhered to the underside of the raised lid, marking a transition from the static presentation to physical interaction with the appliance.

From 34 to 36 seconds, the woman fully opens the lid, supporting it with her left hand to reveal an instructional sticker with purple and orange text. Between 36 and 38 seconds, she reaches her right hand into the drum to touch the central agitator cap while looking inside. Finally, at 38 seconds, the camera cuts to a close-up interior view showing the white agitator with a blue center as her gloved hand approaches it against the perforated metal wall.

From 42 to 44 seconds, the camera cuts from a close-up back to a medium shot of the woman. She leans forward over the open washing machine, using both gloved hands to grip the white central agitator inside the drum. She appears to twist or remove the component while looking down into the appliance, marking the start of the physical removal process.

From 44 to 46 seconds, the camera zooms in as gloved hands twist the top ring of the agitator. Between 46 and 48 seconds, the white ring cap is lifted off, revealing the hollow interior of the central dispenser. At 48 seconds, the woman holds the removed cap while speaking. Finally, from 50 to 52 seconds, she leans over the machine and presses the white plastic ring cap back onto the central dispenser column inside the drum.

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
